method: Within-inscription word-ORDER position permutation null (>=2000 draws, one-sided) testing whether A-initial words occupy the inscription-INITIAL (first-word) position more than chance; cross-site held-out per-site tests + leave-one-site-out on Haghia Triada. LB (DĀMOS) positive control with planted heading bias (DETECT) and position-randomized false-positive arm. L2/L3 positional statistics only; anonymous signs; no phonetics/meaning/reading.
result: A-initial words occupy the inscription-initial (first-word) position far above chance globally (p_A_first=0.1446 vs null mean 0.0790, p=0.0 across 2000 draws; observed exceeds the null maximum of 0.1108). The control axis confirms directionality: p_A_last=0.0458 is BELOW the base A-rate (0.0795), i.e. A-initial words are initial-biased, not final-biased, as a heading-marker hypothesis predicts. The effect is robust at the largest site (Haghia Triada, p=0.0005) and at Khania (p=0.0075), and survives leave-one-site-out of Haghia Triada (p=0.0). However only 2 of 5 testable sites reach individual significance (Knossos, Phaistos, Zakros do not; Zakros in fact trends reverse, p=1.0 with obs below null), so the cross-site robustness bar (>=3 significant sites) is NOT met. Verdict: SITE_LOCAL.
key_findings: GLOBAL heading-position signal is strong and real: A-initial words are the FIRST word of an inscription 14.46% of the time vs a within-inscription position-permutation null mean of 7.90% (p=0.0 over 2000 draws; observed exceeds the null maximum of 0.1108). This is a document-structural (initial-position) role for the A- class, purely positional.

Directionality is correct on the CONTROL axis: p_A_last (A-initial word in FINAL position) = 4.58%, BELOW the base A-rate of 7.95%. A-initial words are initial-biased, not final-biased — consistent with a heading/label role and inconsistent with a generic frequency artifact.

The effect is driven by the two largest sites: Haghia Triada (n=181, p=0.0005) and Khania (n=67, p=0.0075) are individually significant in the initial-biased direction. It SURVIVES leave-one-site-out of Haghia Triada (p=0.0).

Cross-site robustness is only PARTIAL: only 2 of 5 testable sites (>=15 qualifying inscriptions) reach individual significance. Knossos (p=0.33) and Phaistos (p=0.30) trend in the right direction but are underpowered; Zakros (n=34) trends REVERSE (p=1.0, obs 0.029 below null 0.099) — a genuine site heterogeneity that blocks the CROSS_SITE_ROBUST verdict.

POSITIVE CONTROL PASSED: the machinery DETECTS a planted heading bias on LB (DĀMOS) pseudo-inscriptions (p=0.0, correct initial-biased direction) and has a false-positive rate of 0.033 (<=0.10) on position-randomized pseudo-inscriptions across 30 sets. The null is calibrated by construction.
successor_hypotheses: E035: Why does Zakros REVERSE the heading signal (A-initial words AVOID the first position)? Test whether Zakros uses a different document-opening convention (e.g. a different opening sign-class as heading marker) — a comparative site-level document-structure contrast, L2/L3.

E036: Is the heading role of A-initial words SPECIFIC to the A- class, or shared by other common word-initial signs? Run the same position-permutation test for every frequent initial sign (top-N by base rate) to build a positional-profile signature of which sign-classes are heading-biased.

E037: Does the A-initial-first-word effect concentrate on specific SUPPORT types (Tablet vs other)? E033 found A-words depleted on ledger entries; test whether A-initial-first-word is a TABLET-heading phenomenon specifically (support x position interaction, L2/L3).

E038: Combine E033 (A-words are non-entry/label words) with E034 (A-words are inscription-initial): test whether A-initial-first-word inscriptions are disproportionately NON-ledger (few numerals) — i.e. whether A- marks a heading that opens a non-enumerative document section.

E039: Power the underpowered sites. Knossos/Phaistos trend right but n=24/22; acquire or pool comparable small-site inscriptions to push them past significance and re-test the >=3-site robustness bar.

E040: Test SECOND-position and THIRD-position A-initial rates to characterize the full positional decay profile of the A- class (does the heading bias decay monotonically with word rank, as a true document-initial marker should?).
non_circular: Tokens carry NO phonetic/sound/meaning/reading. All statistics are L2/L3 POSITIONAL (word-in-document order) only. The null permutes word ORDER within each inscription, holding each inscription's word multiset fixed, testing POSITION exclusively — calibrated by construction and verified by the false-positive arm of the positive control (FPR=0.033<=0.10). LB (DĀMOS) is used ONLY as a positive-control benchmark to certify the machinery detects a planted positional effect and does not fire on noise; it is never used to read or validate any Linear A claim. 'A-' and 'heading' are anonymous positional labels; 'heading' means DOCUMENT-INITIAL POSITION only.
deviations: LB (DĀMOS) has NO site/inscription metadata, so the positive control uses a SEEDED pseudo-inscription partition (chunking the flat wordform list into ordered groups of 6 words) as stated in the protocol; this is a benchmark-only construction and does not affect the Linear A test.

The planted-heading-bias DETECT arm forces sign 'A' (a common LB first sign, freq 1287/13562) into initial position in ~45% of pseudo-inscriptions by swapping an existing A-initial word into slot 0; this is the 'plant a heading bias' fallback explicitly permitted by the protocol when no native LB heading sign is identifiable.
No deviations from the frozen metric, null, or verdict rule.
